news.title: HC strikes down Tamil Nadu law giving registrars power to block property deals
news.para: Madras High Court has invalidated a Tamil Nadu amendment to the Registration Act, deeming it unconstitutional. The court ruled that Section 34-C, which allowed sub-registrars to block property registrations based on civil court matters, overstepped their authority. This provision, seen as a revival of earlier struck-down rules, infringed upon citizens' property rights and the doctrine of separation of powers.

news.title: Delhi extends slum rehabilitation cutoff year from 2015 to 2025
news.para: Delhi's slum rehabilitation scheme has been significantly expanded, with the cutoff date for eligibility extended to January 1, 2025. This landmark decision, approved by Home Minister Amit Shah, will now benefit lakhs of slum-dwellers, offering them modern housing with essential facilities. The move aligns with the Delhi Slum and JJ Cluster Rehabilitation and Relocation Policy, 2026, aiming to provide dignified living for families and boost the city's economy.

news.title: OCs for unfit buildings: 15 architects face blacklisting for violations in Haryana
news.para: Authorities are moving to blacklist 15 architects in Gurgaon following a major crackdown on irregularities in issuing occupation certificates. Investigations revealed buildings were certified as complete despite being unfinished, some even at the brickwork stage. This has raised serious questions about the self-certification policy for building approvals, with further penalties and disciplinary actions being considered.

news.title: BMC revives OC amnesty scheme proposal for pre-November 2016 buildings in Mumbai
news.para: Mumbai's civic body is set to re-table an amnesty scheme to grant occupation certificates (OC) to buildings occupied before November 17, 2016. The proposal, initially stalled due to disagreements over carpet area restrictions, aims to provide relief to thousands of families. While awaiting state government's final decision on broader eligibility, BMC seeks to pass the current proposal for immediate benefits to eligible residents in legally approved structures.

news.title: Ahmedabad: Court denies bail to builder Popular Builders’ owner
news.para: A city court has denied bail to Popular Builders' owner Raman Patel in a land-grabbing case. The judge cited Patel's prior conviction for using forged documents and concerns about potential evidence tampering, referencing a Supreme Court observation. Patel, who maintains his innocence, faces multiple land-grabbing allegations involving old transactions. The court deemed it unsuitable to grant him bail at this juncture.

news.title: Mumbai: 450-acre slum cluster in Antop Hill, two others set for redevelopment
news.para: Mumbai's Slum Rehabilitation Authority is set to redevelop massive slum clusters in Antop Hill (450 acres), Jogeshwari East (260 acres), and Bandra East (140 acres). Following a successful pilot project in Juhu Galli, where a Reliance-led consortium won the bid, the SRA aims to accelerate large-scale slum redevelopment. This initiative, part of Housing Policy 2025, seeks to transform Mumbai into a slum-free city by providing dignified homes.

news.title: Haryana rolls out auto mutation system for paperless registry in 24 hours
news.para: Haryana has launched an 'Automated Mutation System and Paperless Registration 2.0' to streamline property transactions. This digital initiative eliminates the need for separate mutation applications post-registration, allowing online downloads of records. Aadhaar-based e-KYC is now mandatory, and applicants can track progress online. The system promises faster approvals, with some mutations processed within 24 hours, significantly improving transparency and citizen convenience.

news.title: Flex offices now part of enterprise real estate strategy: Industry leaders
news.para: Speakers at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru said flexible and managed offices are being adopted by enterprises and GCCs as part of long-term real estate planning, rather than as a short-term workspace option.

news.title: North Bengaluru growth to be driven by infrastructure, office demand: Developers
news.para: Speakers at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru said North Bengaluru is moving from a future-growth market to an active residential corridor, supported by airport connectivity, metro expansion, social infrastructure and rising office demand.

news.title: Senior living demand shifting towards active-ageing communities: Experts
news.para: Speakers at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru said senior living in India is moving beyond the old-age home perception and emerging as a managed residential format built around independence, healthcare support, community and lifestyle services.

news.title: GCCs to drive Bengaluru office market, occupiers seek experience-led workplaces
news.para: Speakers at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru said the city’s next commercial real estate cycle will be driven by GCC expansion, employee experience, sustainability, technology-led asset management and better integration with urban infrastructure.

news.title: Pune: Proposal to prepare draft development plan for merged areas gets general body's nod
news.para: Pune Municipal Corporation's general body has greenlit the preparation of a draft development plan for 23 areas merged in 2021. City engineer Aniruddha Pavaskar is appointed town planning officer for this crucial task.  This move aims to overcome past technical hurdles and ensure timely planning for these expanded territories, with surveys and public hearings forming key steps.

news.title: Premiumisation in Bengaluru housing to continue despite AI-led disruptions: Developers
news.para: Speakers at the ET Realty & Beyond Summit Bengaluru said Bengaluru’s residential demand will continue to be supported by GCC expansion, talent availability, infrastructure development and changing homebuyer aspirations.

news.title: Goa: Karapur protest against housing project enters day 78
news.para: Villagers in Karapur are continuing their 78-day protest against a mega housing project, alleging environmental norm violations.  Under the Karapur Gramastha Ekvoticho Aawaz banner, residents, including resolute women, are staging a sit-in.  A tree-planting drive was initiated on Sunday in cleared areas, with hopes of wildlife returning to the restored ecosystem, as evidenced by a peacock sighting.

news.title: Golden Growth Fund closes maiden real estate AIF at ₹101 crore
news.para: Golden Growth Fund, a category-II real estate-focused AIF, has invested in three South Delhi locations: Anand Niketan, Neeti Bagh and Gulmohar Park. The fund was launched in September 2024 and had an NAV of 160.9 as of March 2026.

news.title: Ahmedabad civic body cannot revoke its own regularisation orders: Gujarat HC
news.para: The Gujarat High Court has ruled that once an authority grants or denies regularization of unauthorized construction by accepting an impact fee, it cannot later revoke that decision. The court quashed an Ahmedabad Municipal Corporation order that canceled a prior regularization, stating the authority lacked jurisdiction for such a review. This decision protects property owners from arbitrary cancellations after regularization.
